changes: Strong threshold 75→72, defensibility gate 11→10, timing>=10 gate added, upper-band calibration instruction

issues.description: Test 2 (dog food India, ₹15L) and Test 3 (HIPAA SaaS, $30K solo) both got 0 penalties and verdict 'promising'. In v5.1/v5.2 they were correctly 'risky' with capitalInsufficient penalty. The capitalInsufficient exception (traction override) or competition rules may be too lenient now.

detail.test2: ₹15L budget, no product yet, but 0 penalties applied. capitalInsufficient should apply — no traction to trigger exception.
detail.test3: $30K solo founder with prototype — capitalInsufficient should apply. Prototype is not 'demonstrated traction'.

issues.description: Test 5 (CLI API tool, 400 DAU) jumped to strong/76. Was promising/68-70 in all prior versions. Team 18 and traction 15 seem inflated — 400 DAU is not $10K MRR-level traction.
issues.detail: Lower threshold (72) + inflated sub-scores = false positive. This case should be promising, not strong.

issues.description: Test 6 scored 67 — still below new threshold of 72. The one case we lowered threshold FOR still didn't reach strong. Stochastic — might reach 72 in other runs.
Timing scores are inflated across the board: 24, 18, 16, 15, 15, 11, 18, 14, 21, 12. Average 16.4 — way above expected range. Several impossible scores (24, 21 on 0-20 scale!).
issues.detail: Model is scoring timing above 20 in some cases (test 1: 24, test 9: 21). This is a scoring constraint violation.

comparison.regression: v5.6 is WORSE than v5.2. Penalty system broke (risky cases now promising). Timing inflation. Test 5 false positive for strong.

verdict: v5.6 REGRESSED. Do not ship. Key problems: (1) capitalInsufficient penalty not applying to cases without real traction, (2) timing scores exceeding 0-20 range, (3) Test 5 false-positive strong, (4) Test 6 still not strong.
